key_insight: Transition-metal substitution turns Ga2O2 monolayers from weak adsorbents into tunable platforms for room-temperature toxic-gas sensing or permanent gas scavenging.
engineering_relevance: These tailored 2D materials could enable selective, reusable sensors for atmospheric monitoring and stronger capture media for hazardous gas detoxification.
method: Performed first-principles DFT calculations on Ga2O2 monolayers substituted with Pd, Zn, Zr, Mo, Ag, Ti, and Pt.
Evaluated structural stability using formation/binding energies, lattice distortion, and molecular dynamics simulations.
Compared adsorption and sensing response against eight toxic industrial gases and three environmental gases.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports negative formation and binding energies for all doped systems, negligible structural distortion, and up to eight orders of magnitude conductivity enhancement for Ag-substituted Ga2O2 under NO adsorption.
watch_item: The results are computational and need experimental validation, especially for long-term device stability, real atmospheric interference, and scalable dopant control.
paper_title: Transition-Metal Tailored $Ga_{2}O_{2}$ Monolayer: From Room-Temperature Gas Sensing to Chemical Scavenging
